19:     # @return [Integer] number of records updated
20:     def sync_reactions_count_for_batch(records)
21:       return 0 if records.empty?
22: 
23:       ids = records.map(&:id)
24: 
25:       transaction do
26:         # Lock the rows to prevent concurrent updates (SKIP LOCKED for non-blocking)
27:         where(id: ids).lock("FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED").load
28: 
29:         # Single grouped query to get correct counts for all records
30:         correct_counts = Reaction
31:           .where(reactable_type: name, reactable_id: ids)
32:           .public_category
33:           .group(:reactable_id)
34:           .count
35: 
36:         # Use Arel for safe SQL generation instead of string interpolation
37:         update_cases = ids.map do |record_id|
38:           count = correct_counts[record_id] || 0
39:           Arel.sql("WHEN #{connection.quote(record_id)} THEN #{count.to_i}")
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Perform batch update using ActiveRecord with sanitized Arel
43:         where(id: ids).update_all(
44:           Arel.sql(
45:             "public_reactions_count = CASE id #{update_cases.join(' ')} END",
46:           ),
47:         )
48:       end
49: 
50:       ids.size
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   def sync_reactions_count
55:     # Use transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
56:     self.class.transaction do
57:       self.class.where(id: id).lock("FOR UPDATE").load
58:       correct_count = reactions.public_category.count
59:       self.class.where(id: id).update_all(public_reactions_count: correct_count)
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Thread-safe sync that can be called from background jobs
64:   def sync_reactions_count!
65:     sync_reactions_count
66:     reload
67:   end
68: 
69:   def public_reaction_categories
70:     # Get reaction counts for this reactable
71:     reaction_counts = reaction_counts_for_reactable
72: 
73:     # Filter to only public reaction categories that have reactions
74:     categories_with_counts = reaction_counts.select do |count_data|
75:       category_slug = count_data[:category]
76:       count_data[:count].positive? && ReactionCategory[category_slug]&.visible_to_public?
77:     end
78: 
79:     # Sort by count (descending), then by position for ties, and limit to 3
80:     sorted_categories = categories_with_counts
81:       .sort_by { |count_data| [-count_data[:count], ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]&.position || 99] }
82:       .first(3)
83: 
84:     # Convert back to ReactionCategory objects
85:     sorted_categories.map do |count_data|
86:       ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   private
91: 
92:   def reaction_counts_for_reactable
93:     return [] unless respond_to?(:id)
94: 
95:     Rails.cache.fetch("reaction_counts_for_reactable-#{self.class.name}-#{id}", expires_in: 10.hours) do
96:       reactions.group(:category).count.map do |category, count|
97:         { category: category, count: count }
98:       end
99:     end
100:   end
101: end

1: module Taggable
2:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
3: 
4:   # rubocop:disable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
5:   included do
6:     scope :cached_tagged_with, lambda { |tag|
7:       case tag
8:       when String, Symbol
9:         if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
10:           # Accelerate native `WHERE` filtering escaping sequential regex scans by leveraging GIN index bounds organically dynamically. 
11:           where("tags_array @> ARRAY[?]::text[]", tag.to_s)
12:         else
13:           # In Postgres regexes, the [[:<:]] and [[:>:]] are equivalent to "start of
14:           # word" and "end of word", respectively. They're similar to `\b` in Perl-
15:           # compatible regexes (PCRE), but that matches at either end of a word.
16:           # They're more comparable to how vim's `\<` and `\>` work.
17:           where("cached_tag_list ~ ?", "[[:<:]]#{tag}[[:>:]]")
18:         end
19:       when Array
20:         tag.reduce(self) { |acc, elem| acc.cached_tagged_with(elem) }
21:       when Tag
22:         cached_tagged_with(tag.name)
23:       else
24:         raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tag.inspect}"
25:       end
26:     }
27: 
28:     scope :cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
29:       case tags
30:       when String, Symbol
31:         cached_tagged_with(tags)
32:       when Array
33:         if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
34:           # Accelerate `any` conditions natively utilizing absolute overlap intersections eliminating `OR` sequential bottlenecks dynamically!
35:           where("tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", tags.map(&:to_s))
36:         else
37:           tags
38:             .map { |tag| cached_tagged_with(tag) }
39:             .reduce { |acc, elem| acc.or(elem) }
40:         end
41:       when Tag
42:         cached_tagged_with(tags.name)
43:       else
44:         raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tags.inspect}"
45:       end
46:     }
47: 
48:     # We usually try to avoid using Arel directly like this. However, none of the more
49:     # straight-forward ways of negating the above scope worked:
50:     # 1. A subquery doesn't work because we're not dealing with a simple NOT IN scenario.
51:     # 2. where.not(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).where_values_hash) doesn't work because where_values_hash
52:     #    only works for simple conditions and returns an empty hash in this case.
53:     scope :not_cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
54:       where(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).arel.constraints.reduce(:or).not)
55:     }
56: 
57:     before_save :sync_tags_array
58: 
59:     def sync_tags_array
60:       return unless self.class.column_names.include?("tags_array")
61:       
62:       # We meticulously avoid N+1 evaluation queries by identifying if ActsAsTaggableOn
63:       # tags were explicitly interrogated/mutated. 
64:       # Forem intrinsically instantiates `@tag_list` when validating front matter tags.
65:       tags_were_mutated = cached_tag_list_changed? ||
66:                           instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list) ||
67:                           (cached_tag_list.present? && tags_array.blank?)
68:       
69:       return unless tags_were_mutated
70:       
71:       new_array = if instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list)
72:                     tag_list.to_a
73:                   else
74:                     cached_tag_list.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:blank?).compact
75:                   end
76:                   
77:       self.tags_array = new_array unless tags_array == new_array
78:     end
79:   end
80:   # rubocop:enable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
81: 
82:   # we check tags names aren't too long and don't contain non alphabet characters
83:   def validate_tag_name(tag_list)
84:     tag_list.each do |tag|
85:       new_tag = Tag.new(name: tag)
86:       new_tag.validate_name
87:       new_tag.errors.messages[:name].each { |message| errors.add(:tag, "\"#{tag}\" #{message}") }
88:     end
89:   end
90: end

1: module Articles
2:   class Updater
3:     Result = Struct.new(:success, :article, keyword_init: true)
4: 
5:     def self.call(...)
6:       new(...).call
7:     end
8: 
9:     def initialize(user, article, article_params)
10:       @user = user
11:       @article = article
12:       @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
13:     end
14: 
15:     def call
16:       user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(:article_update)
17:       success = article.update(article_params)
18: 
19:       if success
20:         user.rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:article_update)
21: 
22:         if became_unpublished?
23:           remove_all_notifications
24:           bust_author_profile_cache
25:         end
26: 
27:         send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers if remains_published?
28:         refresh_auto_audience_segments if became_published?
29:         complete_onboarding_first_post if became_published?
30:       end
31: 
32:       Result.new(success: success, article: article.decorate)
33:     end
34: 
35:     private
36: 
37:     attr_reader :user, :article, :article_params
38: 
39:     def normalize_params(original_params)
40:       article_params = original_params.dup
41: 
42:       # updated edited time only if already published and not edited by an admin
43:       update_edited_at = article.user == user && article.published
44: 
45:       # remove published_at values received from a user if an articles was published before (has past published_at)
46:       # published_at will remain as it was in this case
47:       article_params.delete :published_at if article.published_at && !article.scheduled?
48: 
49:       # NOTE: It's surprising that this is article.user and not @user
50:       Articles::Attributes.new(article_params, article.user, organization: article.organization)
51:         .for_update(update_edited_at: update_edited_at)
52:     end
53: 
54:     def refresh_auto_audience_segments
55:       user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
56:     end
57: 
58:     def complete_onboarding_first_post
59:       return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
60: 
61:       user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
62:     end
63: 
64:     def became_published?
65:       article.published? && !article.published_previously_was
66:     end
67: 
68:     def remains_published?
69:       article.published && article.saved_change_to_published.blank?
70:     end
71: 
72:     def became_unpublished?
73:       article.saved_changes["published"] == [true, false]
74:     end
75: 
76:     # If the article has already been published and is only being updated, then we need to create
77:     # mentions and send notifications to mentioned users inline via the Mentions::CreateAll service.
78:     def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers
79:       Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)
80:     end
81: 
82:     # Remove any associated notifications if Article is unpublished
83:     def remove_all_notifications
84:       Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article",
85:                                                       action: "Published")
86:       ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article",
87:                                     action: "Published")
88: 
89:       if article.comments.exists?
90:         Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids,
91:                                 notifiable_type: "Comment")
92:       end
93:       return unless article.mentions.exists?
94: 
95:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.mentions.ids,
96:                               notifiable_type: "Mention")
97:     end
98: 
99:     def bust_author_profile_cache
100:       EdgeCache::BustUser.call(article.user)
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

1: module EdgeCache
2:   class BustArticle
3:     TIMEFRAMES = [
4:       [-> { 1.week.ago }, "week"],
5:       [-> { 1.month.ago }, "month"],
6:       [-> { 1.year.ago }, "year"],
7:       [-> { 5.years.ago }, "infinity"],
8:     ].freeze
9:     def self.call(article)
10:       return unless article
11: 
12:       article.purge
13:       article.user&.purge
14:       article.organization&.purge
15: 
16:       cache_bust = EdgeCache::Bust.new
17: 
18:       bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
19:       bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
20:       bust_user_profile_pages(article)
21:     end
22: 
23:     def self.bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
24:       if article.published_at.to_i > Time.current.to_i
25:         # TODO
26:         cache_bust.call("/")
27:       end
28: 
29:       if article.video.present? && article.published_at.to_i > 10.days.ago.to_i
30:         cache_bust.call("/videos")
31:       end
32: 
33:       TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
34:         next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call)
35:           .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
36: 
37:         cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
38:       end
39: 
40:       if article.published && article.published_at > 1.hour.ago
41:         cache_bust.call("/latest")
42:       end
43: 
44:       cache_bust.call("/") if Article.published.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(4).ids.include?(article.id)
45:     end
46: 
47:     private_class_method :bust_home_pages
48: 
49:     def self.bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
50:       return unless article.published
51: 
52:       article.tag_list.each do |tag|
53:         if article.published_at.to_i > 2.minutes.ago.to_i
54:           cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}/latest")
55:         end
56: 
57:         TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
58:           next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call).cached_tagged_with_any(tag)
59:             .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
60: 
61:           cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
62:           12.times do |i|
63:             cache_bust.call("/api/articles?tag=#{tag}&top=#{i}")
64:           end
65:         end
66: 
67:         next unless rand(2) == 1 &&
68:           Article.published.cached_tagged_with(tag)
69:             .order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(2).ids.include?(article.id)
70: 
71:         cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}")
72:       end
73:     end
74: 
75:     private_class_method :bust_tag_pages
76: 
77:     def self.bust_user_profile_pages(article)
78:       return unless article.user
79: 
80:       EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
81:         article.user.profile_cache_keys,
82:         fallback_paths: article.user.profile_cache_bust_paths,
83:       )
84:     end
85: 
86:     private_class_method :bust_user_profile_pages
87:   end
88: end

1: module Mentions
2:   # This class creates mentions + associated notifications for Articles and Comments.
3:   # This class will check to see if there are any @-mentions in the post, and will
4:   # create the associated mentions inline if necessary.
5:   class CreateAll
6:     def self.call(...)
7:       new(...).call
8:     end
9: 
10:     def initialize(notifiable)
11:       @notifiable = notifiable
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call
15:       mentioned_users = users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
16: 
17:       delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(mentioned_users)
18:       create_mentions_for(mentioned_users)
19:     end
20: 
21:     private
22: 
23:     def users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
24:       mentioned_usernames = extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
25: 
26:       collect_existing_users(mentioned_usernames)
27:         .then do |existing_mentioned_users|
28:           reject_notifiable_author(existing_mentioned_users)
29:         end
30:     end
31: 
32:     def collect_existing_users(usernames)
33:       User.registered.where(username: usernames)
34:     end
35: 
36:     def create_mentions_for(users)
37:       users.each { |user| create_mention_for(user) }
38:     end
39: 
40:     def extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
41:       # The "mentioned-user" css is added by Html::Parser#user_link_if_exists
42:       doc = Nokogiri::HTML(notifiable.processed_html)
43: 
44:       # Remove any mentions that are embedded within any liquid tags
45:       non_liquid_tag_mentions = doc.css(".mentioned-user").reject do |tag|
46:         tag.ancestors(".liquid-comment").any? ||
47:           tag.ancestors("[class^=ltag]").any? ||
48:           tag.ancestors("[class*=' ltag']").any?
49:       end
50: 
51:       non_liquid_tag_mentions.map { |link| link.text.delete("@").downcase }
52:     end
53: 
54:     def reject_notifiable_author(users)
55:       users.reject { |user| authored_by?(user, notifiable) }
56:     end
57: 
58:     def authored_by?(user, notifiable)
59:       user.id == notifiable.user_id
60:     end
61: 
62:     def delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(users)
63:       mentions = notifiable.mentions.where.not(user_id: users).destroy_all
64:       Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: mentions.map(&:id), notifiable_type: "Mention") if mentions.present?
65:     end
66: 
67:     def user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
68:       user.notifications.exists?(notifiable_id: notifiable.id, notifiable_type: "Comment")
69:     end
70: 
71:     def create_mention_for(user)
72:       # Do not create additional notifications for being mentioned in a comment.
73:       if notifiable.is_a?(Comment) && user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
74:         return
75:       end
76: 
77:       # The mentionable_type is the model that created the mention, the user is the user to be mentioned.
78:       mention = Mention.create(user_id: user.id, mentionable_id: notifiable.id,
79:                                mentionable_type: notifiable.class.name)
80: 
81:       # If notifiable is an Article, we need to create the notification for the mention immediately so
82:       # that the notification exists in the database before we attempt to create other Article-related notifications.
83:       # However, if notifiable is a Comment, we can create the notification for the mention in the background.
84:       case notifiable
85:       when Article
86:         Notification.send_mention_notification_without_delay(mention)
87:       when Comment
88:         Notification.send_mention_notification(mention)
89:       end
90: 
91:       mention
92:     end
93: 
94:     attr_reader :notifiable
95:   end
96: end

content.text:
## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

This is **Forem**, the Rails application that powers dev.to. It is a conventional Rails monolith with a Preact frontend in transition.

Key map for a teammate:

- Central content model: **`app/models/article.rb`** (1,858 lines) — the thing authors publish.
- Shared behavior for Article lives in concerns under **`app/models/concerns/`** and in service objects under **`app/services/articles/`** and **`app/services/articles/feeds/`**.
- Background work is in **`app/workers/`**, especially **`app/workers/articles/`**.
- Request handling for articles: **`app/controllers/articles_controller.rb`**, **`app/controllers/stories_controller.rb`**, **`app/controllers/stories/feeds_controller.rb`**, and API controllers under **`app/controllers/concerns/api/articles_controller.rb`**.
- Caching logic: **`app/services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb`**.
- Notifications/mentions: **`app/models/notification.rb`**, **`app/services/notifications/`**, **`app/services/mentions/create_all.rb`**.
- Tests to trust: **`spec/models/article_spec.rb`**, **`spec/models/article_destroy_spec.rb`**, **`spec/requests/articles/`**.

## Step 2 — Map the Article contract and what it is built on

**Model and shared pieces**

| Piece | File:line | What it adds to Article |
|---|---|---|
| `Article` | `app/models/article.rb:1` | The central content model: posts, statuses, fullscreen embeds. |
| `ApplicationRecord` | `app/models/application_record.rb:1` | Base Rails model inherited by Article. |
| `LiquidEmbeddable` | `app/models/concerns/liquid_embeddable.rb:1` | `has_many :liquid_embed_references` (polymorphic `as: :record`, `dependent: :destroy`) and callbacks that queue `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker` and sync metadata on publish changes. |
| `Reactable` | `app/models/concerns/reactable.rb:1` | `has_many :reactions` (polymorphic `as: :reactable`, `dependent: :destroy`) plus reaction-count syncing. |
| `Taggable` | `app/models/concerns/taggable.rb:1` | `cached_tagged_with` / `cached_tagged_with_any` scopes and `sync_tags_array` before-save hook. |
| `UserSubscriptionSourceable` | `app/models/concerns/user_subscription_sourceable.rb:1` | `has_many :user_subscriptions` (polymorphic, `dependent: :nullify`). |
| `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle` | `app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb:1` | Algolia index config and `indexable?` / `indexable_changed?` guards. |
| `WebpageTrackable` | `app/models/concerns/webpage_trackable.rb:1` | `has_many :webpage_references` (polymorphic, `dependent: :delete_all`), plus sync and linked-domain score update callbacks. |

**Key associations on Article**

| Association | File:line | Teardown semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `belongs_to :user` | `app/models/article.rb:47` | User owns articles; `User.has_many :articles, dependent: :destroy` means user deletion destroys articles. |
| `belongs_to :organization, optional: true` | `app/models/article.rb:46` | `Organization.has_many :articles, dependent: :nullify`. |
| `belongs_to :collection, optional: true` | `app/models/article.rb:44` | `Collection.has_many :articles, dependent: :nullify`. |
| `belongs_to :subforem, optional: true` | `app/models/article.rb:48` | `Subforem.has_many :articles, dependent: :nullify`. |
| `has_one :discussion_lock, dependent: :delete` | `app/models/article.rb:168` | Deleted synchronously with article. |
| `has_many :mentions, as: :mentionable, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:170` | Deleted synchronously with article. |
| `has_many :comments, as: :commentable, dependent: :nullify` | `app/models/article.rb:171` | **Not destroyed** — comments are nullified. This is a teardown gotcha. |
| `has_many :context_notifications, as: :context, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:172` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :ai_audits, as: :affected_content, dependent: :nullify` | `app/models/article.rb:173` | Nullified, not destroyed. |
| `has_many :feed_events, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:176` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :notification_subscriptions, as: :notifiable, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:177` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :notifications, as: :notifiable, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:178` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :page_views, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:179` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_one :article_activity, dependent: :delete` | `app/models/article.rb:180` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :polls, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/article.rb:183` | Destroyed (poll options/votes cascade). |
| `has_many :profile_pins, as: :pinnable, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:184` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :rating_votes, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/article.rb:187` | Destroyed (uses counter_culture). |
| `has_many :tag_adjustments` | `app/models/article.rb:188` | **No dependent option** — teardown must handle these explicitly. |
| `has_many :context_notes, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/article.rb:189` | Deleted synchronously. |
| `has_many :trend_memberships, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/article.rb:192` | Destroyed. |
| `has_many :concept_memberships, as: :record, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/article.rb:195` | Destroyed. |
| `has_many :reactions, as: :reactable, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/concerns/reactable.rb:5` | Via concern — destroyed. |
| `has_many :liquid_embed_references, as: :record, dependent: :destroy` | `app/models/concerns/liquid_embeddable.rb:5` | Via concern — destroyed. |
| `has_many :webpage_references, as: :record, dependent: :delete_all` | `app/models/concerns/webpage_trackable.rb:5` | Via concern — deleted. |
| `has_many :user_subscriptions, as: :user_subscription_sourceable, dependent: :nullify` | `app/models/concerns/user_subscription_sourceable.rb:7` | Via concern — nullified. |

**What a teammate must know before changing the contract**

1. **`comments` are `dependent: :nullify`**, not destroyed. Any teardown rework must decide whether orphaned comments are intentional and whether their notifications/mentions should be cleaned up separately.
2. **`tag_adjustments` has no `dependent` option** and references `article_id` directly. Article deletion currently leaves these rows unless handled elsewhere.
3. **Counter caches** (`comments_count`, `public_reactions_count`, etc.) skip Active Record callbacks; do not rely on `after_update_commit` for counter-increment-driven behavior.
4. **Polymorphic associations** (`reactions`, `mentions`, `notifications`, `notification_subscriptions`, `context_notifications`, `concept_memberships`, `profile_pins`, `liquid_embed_references`, `webpage_references`, `user_subscriptions`) all rely on `Article`’s class name and id.
5. **Cached serialized entities** (`cached_user`, `cached_organization`) and `reading_list_document` are maintained by model hooks and a PostgreSQL trigger; changing teardown may require cache invalidation.

## Step 3 — Trace how an article and its attached data are handled on change

### Create path

1. **Request entry**: `ArticlesController#create` (`app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:150`) or `Api::ArticlesController#create` (`app/controllers/concerns/api/articles_controller.rb:63`).
2. **Service**: `Articles::Creator#call` (`app/services/articles/creator.rb:26`) builds and saves the Article.
3. **Model callbacks fire on save** (`app/models/article.rb:295-346`):
   - `before_validation`: markdown processing, slug creation, published date, title normalization, subforem default.
   - `before_save`: cached entities, base scores, video duration, caches, language detection.
   - `after_save`: conditional spam check, cache bust, collection cleanup, social image, context notes.
   - `after_commit`: embedding generation, score calc, collection touch, image enrichment, code-block detection, org-page recompilation.
4. **After creation, if published**: `Articles::PublishWorker` (`app/workers/articles/publish_worker.rb:7`) later picks it up to send Slack messages and follower/mention notifications.

### Update path

1. **Request entry**: `ArticlesController#update` or `Api::ArticlesController#update` (`app/controllers/concerns/api/articles_controller.rb:76`).
2. **Service**: `Articles::Updater#call` (`app/services/articles/updater.rb:15`) runs `article.update(article_params)`.
3. **Model callbacks fire** as above, plus `after_update_commit` hooks:
   - `update_notifications` (`app/models/article.rb:321`)
   - `update_notification_subscriptions` (`app/models/article.rb:324`)
   - `update_dependent_embeds_if_key_info_changed` (`app/models/article.rb:1844`)
   - `regenerate_summary_if_content_changed` (`app/models/article.rb:1069`)
4. **Service-level side effects** after the update (`app/services/articles/updater.rb:19-30`):
   - `remove_all_notifications` and `bust_author_profile_cache` if became unpublished.
   - `Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)` if remains published.
   - `refresh_auto_audience_segments` and `complete_onboarding_first_post` if became published.

### Destroy path

1. **Request entry**: `ArticlesController#destroy` (`app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:207`) or `Users::DeleteArticles#call` (`app/services/users/delete_articles.rb:5`) for user deletion cascades.
2. **Service**: `Articles::Destroyer#call` (`app/services/articles/destroyer.rb:5`):
   - Caches comment ids (because comments will be nullified).
   - Calls `article.destroy!`.
   - Removes notifications for the article, its now-orphaned comments, and its mentions.
   - Busts author cache.
3. **Model callbacks during destroy**:
   - `before_destroy :before_destroy_actions, prepend: true` (`app/models/article.rb:313`) busts cache and enqueues `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker` for sibling articles.
   - Active Record runs all `dependent:` associations:
     - `:delete_all`/`:delete` happen inline.
     - `:destroy` on `polls` and `rating_votes` runs their callbacks inline.
     - `comments` are **nullified inline** (no callbacks on comments).
   - `after_destroy_commit` from `WebpageTrackable` queues `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker`.
   - `after_commit :recompile_organization_pages, on: %i[create update destroy]` (`app/models/article.rb:345`) runs after destroy.

### Inline vs asynchronous summary

**Inline (within the transaction or request)**:
- Validations and `before_validation`/`before_save` hooks.
- `dependent: :delete_all`, `:delete`, `:nullify`, `:destroy` associations.
- `after_save` hooks (`bust_cache` does `purge` + `async_bust` inline; the actual cache bust is async).
- `Articles::Destroyer` notification cleanup.

**Asynchronous (Sidekiq)**:
- `Articles::BustCacheWorker` (`app/workers/articles/bust_cache_worker.rb:3`) from `async_bust`.
- `Articles::BustMultipleCachesWorker` (`app/workers/articles/bust_multiple_caches_worker.rb:9`) from `before_destroy_actions`.
- `Articles::ScoreCalcWorker` (`app/workers/articles/score_calc_worker.rb:7`) from `async_score_calc`.
- `Images::SocialImageWorker` (`app/workers/images/social_image_worker.rb:7`) from `generate_social_image`.
- `Articles::HandleSpamWorker` (`app/workers/articles/handle_spam_worker.rb`) from `create_conditional_autovomits`.
- `Articles::EnrichImageAttributesWorker` from `enrich_image_attributes`.
- `Articles::DetectCodeBlockLanguagesWorker` from `detect_code_block_languages`.
- `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker` from `enqueue_generate_embedding`.
- `Articles::UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker` from `update_dependent_embeds_if_key_info_changed`.
- `Articles::GenerateSummaryWorker` from `regenerate_summary_if_content_changed`.
- `Articles::GenerateContextNoteWorker` from `generate_context_notes`.
- `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker` from `LiquidEmbeddable`.
- `SyncWebpageReferencesWorker` and `LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker` from `WebpageTrackable`.
- `Organizations::RecompilePagesWorker` from `recompile_organization_pages`.
- `Notifications::NotifiableActionWorker` for follower notifications.
- `Articles::PublishWorker` for scheduled published-article notification fan-out.

## Step 4 — Audit every dependent of the Article contract

The Sense index reports **~200 direct callers of `Article` across 79 files** and **340 affected symbols total**. Below are the dependents grouped by area, with file:line and a one-phrase usage note. This covers the primary, non-obvious, and indirect dependents; the exhaustive set of files is available via the static graph.

### Request handling

| Dependent | File:line | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `Admin::ArticlesController` | `app/controllers/admin/articles_controller.rb:2` | Lists, pins, unpins, updates articles. |
| `Admin::ToolsController#handle_article_cache` | `app/controllers/admin/tools_controller.rb:58` | Manual cache bust for an article. |
| `Admin::WelcomeController` | `app/controllers/admin/welcome_controller.rb:2` | Creates welcome thread articles. |
| `Ahoy::EmailClicksController#record_feed_event` | `app/controllers/ahoy/email_clicks_controller.rb:71` | Records feed events tied to articles. |
| `ArticleApprovalsController#create` | `app/controllers/article_approvals_controller.rb:2` | Approves an article. |
| `ArticlesController#feed` | `app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:28` | Renders article feed. |
| `ArticlesController#new` | `app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:77` | Prepares new article form. |
| `ArticlesController#preview` | `app/controllers/articles_controller.rb:114` | Previews article markdown. |

## Step 5 — Trace how article identity and visibility are guarded

| Guard | File:line | What it checks |
|---|---|---|
| `Article#published?` | generated from `published` boolean | Whether `published` is true. |
| `Article.published` scope | `app/models/article.rb:411` | `published: true` and `published_at <= now`. |
| `Article#scheduled?` | `app/models/article.rb:663` | `published_at? && published_at.future?`. |
| `Notification.send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers` | `app/models/notification.rb:65` | `notifiable.is_a?(Article) && notifiable.published? && notifiable.type_of == "full_post"`. |
| `Article#async_score_calc` | `app/models/article.rb:1123` | `return if !published? || destroyed?`. |
| `Article#generate_social_image` | `app/models/article.rb:1673` | Requires `published` and title/published_at/social_image change. |
| `Article#enqueue_generate_embedding` | `app/models/article.rb:1814` | Requires `score >= home_feed_minimum_score`, content changed, embedding column present. |
| `Article#recompile_organization_pages` | `app/models/article.rb:1737` | Requires `is_published || was_published`. |
| `Articles::BustCacheWorker#perform` | `app/workers/articles/bust_cache_worker.rb:5` | `Article.find_by(id: article_id); return unless article`. |
| `EdgeCache::BustArticle.call` | `app/services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb:10` | `return unless article`. |
| `Articles::PublishWorker#perform` | `app/workers/articles/publish_worker.rb:9` | Selects `published: true` articles with past `published_at` missing `context_notifications_published`. |
| `AlgoliaSearchable::SearchableArticle#indexable` | `app/models/concerns/algolia_searchable/searchable_article.rb:42` | `published && score.positive? && published_at < Time.current`. |
| `Article#skip_indexing?` | `app/models/article.rb:901` | Inverse guard for crawlers: unpublished, low score, or too old. |

**Pattern summary**: most background code does a `find_by` + early `return` for identity, then checks `published?` or the `published` scope for visibility. The most important centralized guard is `Notification.send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers` at `app/models/notification.rb:65`, which also restricts to `type_of == "full_post"`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of changing the Article teardown/change contract

Ranked by risk:

| Rank | Area | Risk | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Comments** | **Highest** | `dependent: :nullify` means comments survive article destruction. `Articles::Destroyer` pre-caches comment ids and manually removes their notifications, but comment records, their reactions, and nested replies remain. Any change to Article teardown must decide whether this is intentional. |
| **2** | **Notifications / Mentions / Subscriptions** | **High** | `Notification.remove_all`, `Mentions::CreateAll`, `NotificationSubscription`, and `ContextNotification` are tightly coupled to article lifecycle. Changing `dependent:` or destroy order will break notification cleanup. |
| **3** | **Counter caches** | **High** | `counter_culture` on User/Organization and Rails counter caches skip AR callbacks. If teardown moves counts, counters will drift. |
| **4** | **Caching / EdgeCache** | **High** | `EdgeCache::BustArticle`, `before_destroy_actions`, `async_bust`, and `recompile_organization_pages` all rely on article state at destroy/update time. Changing when/what is destroyed changes what gets busted. |
| **5** | **Feeds and ranking** | **Medium-High** | Feed services query `Article.published` and rely on `published_at`, `score`, `hotness_score`. If teardown changes published state or score recalculation timing, feeds become stale. |
| **6** | **Search / Embeddings** | **Medium-High** | `AlgoliaSearchable`, `pg_search`, `has_neighbors :semantic_embedding`, and `GenerateArticleEmbeddingWorker` depend on article existence and published state. |
| **7** | **Polymorphic associations** | **Medium** | `reactions`, `mentions`, `notifications`, `notification_subscriptions`, `context_notifications`, `profile_pins`, `concept_memberships`, `liquid_embed_references`, `webpage_references`, `user_subscriptions` all use `Article` class name. Changing polymorphic cleanup order or class name breaks them. |
| **8** | **Tag adjustments** | **Medium** | `tag_adjustments` has **no `dependent`** option. Destroying an article leaves orphaned `TagAdjustment` rows unless explicitly handled. |
| **9** | **AI audits / Context notes / Article activity** | **Medium** | `ai_audits` are nullified; `article_activity`, `context_notes` are deleted. Changing teardown may leave or delete data unexpectedly. |
| **10** | **Social images / Embeds / Webpage refs** | **Medium** | `Images::SocialImageWorker`, `Articles::UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker`, `SyncLiquidEmbedReferencesWorker`, `SyncWebpageReferencesWorker` depend on article remaining findable while jobs run. |
| **11** | **Onboarding / Badges** | **Low-Medium** | First-post badge and onboarding checklist depend on `article.published?` transitions. |
| **12** | **Analytics / Metrics** | **Low-Medium** | Analytics queries and `Metrics::RecordDailyUsageWorker` aggregate article rows; teardown changes counts. |
